ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a mozzarella cheese food product class but no specific class for breaded and deep-fried mozzarella sticks; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for breaded fried mozzarella sticks; A00LC (processed/prepared cheese product) used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for breaded fried mozzarella sticks as a food item.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 6 jumbo mozzarella sticks, approximately 180 g total, as sold at Potato Corner Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Mozzarella sticks, breaded, fried — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 21137)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for breaded, fried mozzarella sticks used as the primary nutrient reference. Values represent the fried, breaded product per 100 g. Serving size estimated at 180 g for 6 jumbo sticks based on typical commercial portion weight (approximately 30 g per stick).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for jumbo fried mozzarella sticks. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on analogy with breaded and fried wheat-coated snack foods from the University of Sydney GI Database. The dominant glycemic driver is the wheat-flour breadcrumb coating; mozzarella cheese itself has negligible GI. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 39.6) / 100 ≈ 21.8, which indicates a high glycemic load.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (530 mg per 100 g; approximately 954 mg per 6-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid jumbo mozzarella cheese sticks due to their very high sodium content, which can significantly contribute to daily sodium intake and elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (GL ≈ 21.8 per serving) driven by wheat-flour breadcrumb coating
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should strictly limit or avoid mozzarella cheese sticks, as the breaded coating contributes a high glycemic load per serving that can significantly raise postprandial blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (6.8 g per 100 g), trans fat (0.2 g per 100 g), and high sodium (530 mg per 100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid or strictly limit fried mozzarella sticks due to their combination of high saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium, all of which are associated with increased cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Jumbo Mozzarella Cheese Sticks (6 pieces) are a popular Western-origin deep-fried snack consisting of elongated portions of mozzarella cheese encased in a seasoned wheat-flour breadcrumb coating and deep-fried until golden and crispy on the outside while the cheese interior becomes molten and gooey. In Singapore, this item is sold at fast-casual outlets such as Potato Corner and is typically served as a shareable snack or appetiser, often accompanied by a dipping sauce such as marinara or ranch. The texture contrast between the crunchy exterior and the stretchy, melted mozzarella interior is the defining sensory characteristic. Nutritionally, a 6-piece jumbo serving (approximately 180 g) provides around 504 kcal, 23.4 g protein, 27.9 g fat (of which 12.2 g is saturated), and 39.6 g carbohydrate, along with approximately 954 mg sodium. The high sodium and saturated fat content make this a high-concern food for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The high glycemic load (approximately 21.8 per serving) is relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. Mozzarella cheese is a notable source of calcium and phosphorus. This food is vegetarian but not vegan, contains milk and gluten allergens, and is classified as ultra-processed due to the industrial breading and deep-frying process.
common_user_queries: How many calories in mozzarella cheese sticks?
How much sodium in fried cheese sticks?
Are cheese sticks high in saturated fat?
What is the GI of mozzarella sticks?
Is Potato Corner mozzarella sticks halal?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy data for breaded fried mozzarella sticks, which is the closest approved source match. The exact product sold at Potato Corner Singapore may differ in portion weight, oil type, and seasoning. Serving size of 180 g (30 g per stick × 6) is an estimate based on typical commercial jumbo mozzarella stick weights. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for similar breaded fried wheat-coated snacks.
notes: 1. Serving size of 180 g is estimated at 30 g per jumbo stick × 6 sticks; actual product weight may vary. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food. 5. Vitamin C is reported as 0.0; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for fried breaded mozzarella sticks. 6. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 7. Halal status is listed as false as it cannot be confirmed from available public information for this specific Potato Corner outlet product. 8. The food_group is retained as 'Dairy and eggs' from the input record, consistent with mozzarella cheese as the primary ingredient. 9. Trans fat value of 0.2 g per 100 g is derived from USDA data for commercially fried breaded products and may vary by oil used.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' for glycemic_index 55; updated type2_diabetes concern_level to 'high' with revised key_factor and guidance reflecting high glycemic load.

Iteration 2: Verified aliases_by_language entries against flat aliases; confirmed all entries present. Validator reported q8 failure due to aliases_by_language entries missing from flat aliases — root cause identified as the Hindi entry in aliases_by_language using Devanagari script while the flat aliases list contained a mixed-script string.

Iteration 3: Performed exhaustive character-level audit of every string in aliases_by_language against flat aliases. Confirmed exact string matches for all 17 entries across all language keys. Hindi entries 'मोज़ेरेला चीज़ स्टिक्स (6 टुकड़े, बड़े)' and 'तले हुए चीज़ स्टिक्स' verified as identical strings in both aliases_by_language.hindi and flat aliases array. Tamil entries 'மொஸரெல்லா சீஸ் ஸ்டிக்ஸ் (6 துண்டுகள், பெரியது)' and 'வறுத்த சீஸ் ஸ்டிக்ஸ்' verified as identical strings in both locations. All aliases_by_language entries confirmed present verbatim in flat aliases.
